# UC-17.6.7 · Akamai Guardicore Segmentation Policy Change Audit Trail (`guardicore:policy`)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Audit, Governance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.*

---

## Description

Policy drift increases risk; auditing who relaxed rules supports least-privilege governance.

## Value

Forensics can tie breaches to a specific policy edit.

## Implementation

Install the add-on, configure API polling intervals appropriate to API limits, and map assets to CMDB for enrichment lookups.

## SPL

```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:policy" earliest=-30d
| stats count values(change_type) as changes by admin_user, policy_name
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.Change
  by Change.user, Change.object, Change.action span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Timeline, table.

## Known False Positives

Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.

## References

- [Splunkbase — Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7426)
